# Index Funds

<br>

A low-cost, broad-market index fund is the closest thing personal finance
has to a free lunch. You own a tiny slice of thousands of companies, pay
almost nothing in fees, and beat most active managers over long periods
[@bogle2017little].

  - Expense ratios: why a 0.04% vs. 1% fee matters
  - Total-market vs. S&P 500 vs. international
  - ETFs vs. mutual funds
